Q8 Not checked
General Aptitude • INSTRUMENTATION ENGINEERING • Beginner • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of phrases given below should replace the phrase printed in bold type to make it grammatically correct?
Despite of their differences on matters of the principles, they all agree on the demand of hike in salary.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
“Despite their” in place of “Despite of their”. The preposition "of" is unnecessary after "despite".

Q9 Not checked
General Aptitude • INSTRUMENTATION ENGINEERING • Beginner • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Choose an option, which can be substituted for the given phrase:
Something that can not be imitated.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Inimitable means incapable of being duplicated. The word duplicity means dishonest behavior that is intended to trick someone. Something that is impossible to explain is called inexplicable and something that is impossible to avoid or prevent is termed as inevitable.

Q20 Not checked

Not attempted

The additional time available for the performance of an activity in PERT and CPM if all activities starts at their earliest start time is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Free float is the part of total float which can be used without affecting the float of succeeding activities. So that succeeding activity can be started by their earliest start time.

Q33 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) regarding iron-carbon alloys is/are INCORRECT?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a, b)
• Tempered martensite is relatively ductile than martensite.
• Martensite steels are most brittle.
Hence, statement (a) and (b) are INCORRECT statements.

Q34 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are method(s) normally used for the production of metal power?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
The methods normally used for the production of metal powder are :
• Atomization
• Reduction
• Comminution
• Electrolytic deposition
• Pulverization
• Mechanical alloying
